narration: Import the file system module by assigning it to a variable named fs.
Use fs.readFile to read a file, passing the file path as the first parameter.
Pass the encoding option (e.g., 'utf8') as the second parameter to fs.readFile.
Define a callback function as the third parameter with 'error' and 'data' arguments.
Inside the callback, use console.log to print the data if the file is read successfully.
Use fs.writeFile to create a new file or overwrite an existing one.
Pass the file path as the first parameter to fs.writeFile.
Pass the content string to be written as the second parameter.
Define a callback function for fs.writeFile to handle errors and confirm data saving.
Use fs.appendFile to add data to the end of an existing file instead of overwriting it.
Use fs.unlink to delete a file by passing the file path and a callback function.
Inside the unlink callback, log a confirmation message indicating the file was deleted.
